analysis.problem: Разпространението на хантавируса сред пътници на круизен кораб
analysis.root_cause: Отсъствие на ефективни мерки за профилактика и контрол на болестите при международните пътувания
leverage_points: Световната здравна организация (WHO) и местните власти имат най-голям ефект върху проблема
Уучастниците в международните пътувания и туристическите оператори

proposed_actions.action: Увеличаване на контрола и профилактичните мерки при международните пътувания
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Снижаване на броя на хантавирусните случаи сред пътници с 50% през следващите 6 месеца

proposed_actions.action: Разработка на нови протоколи за реакция при изключителни ситуации, включително и разпространение на болести
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Създаване на протоколите в рамките на 3 месеца и тяхното внедряване в международните аерогари и пристанища

evidence: Докладът на WHO за изразходване на хантавируса сред пътници на круизен кораб МВ Хондий
Информацията за хантавируса и начина, по който се разпространява, предоставена от WHO
analysis.generalization: Проблемът е свързан с домейна на цивилизацията, тъй като международните пътувания и туризъм са важни за глобалната икономика и култура, но също така могат да допринесат за разпространението на болестите
analysis.summary: Разпространението на хантавируса сред пътници на круизен кораб и необходимостта от увеличаване на контрола и профилактичните мерки за предотвратяване на болестите при международните пътувания

raw_items.title: WHO’s response to hantavirus cases linked to a cruise ship
raw_items.summary:
Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General, briefed media today on a cluster of hantavirus cases linked to a cruise ship, the MV Hondius.

Eight cases have been reported so far, including three deaths. Five of the 8 cases have been confirmed as hantavirus.

The hantavirus involved is the Andes virus, the only species known to be capable of limited transmission between humans, linked to close and prolonged contact.

Describing the situation, Dr Tedros said, &ld

raw_items.title: Joint call by the President of the ICRC, the Director-General of WHO and the International President of MSF
raw_items.summary: Ten years ago, the UN Security Council unanimously adopted Resolution 2286 on health care in armed conflicts. The situation is even worse compared to 10 years ago. Today, we mark not an achievement – we mark a failure.  As violence affecting medical facilities, transport and personnel continues unabated, the harm this resolution sought to prevent has not diminished. It has continued and, in many contexts, intensified.  As the heads of the International Committee of the Red Cross (ICRC), the Worl

raw_items.title: WHO Member States agree to extend negotiations on Pathogen Access and Benefit Sharing annex
raw_items.summary:
Member States of the World Health Organization (WHO) have progressed work on the Pathogen Access and Benefit Sharing (PABS) annex, a key part of the WHO Pandemic Agreement, and today agreed additional time was needed to finalize the framework for ensuring a better, more equitable, response to future pandemics.

Countries today ended the resumed session of the sixth meeting of the Intergovernmental Working Group (IGWG) on the WHO Pandemic Agreement in Geneva, focused on the PABS system. T

raw_items.title: Australia becomes the 30th country to eliminate trachoma as a public health problem
raw_items.summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) has validated Australia for eliminating trachoma as a public health problem, marking a significant milestone in the health of indigenous peoples and in global efforts to combat neglected tropical diseases (NTDs). Trachoma, the world’s leading infectious cause of blindness, no longer represents a public health problem in the country.

raw_items.title: Ethiopian woman's joy at rare quintuplets after 12 years trying for a baby
raw_items.summary: The woman, 35, says she was praying for a baby and was "overjoyed" to be "blessed with five at once".

raw_items.summary: Some passengers potentially exposed to hantavirus have already taken flights to numerous countries.

raw_items.title: What is hantavirus and how does it spread?
raw_items.summary: The Andes strain of the virus, which can rarely be passed from person to person,  has been confirmed in some passengers.
